claims.claim: worker.py:1170 _heartbeat_monitor_handler monitors heartbeat for processing time + timeout
claims.found: async def _heartbeat_monitor_handler(self): ... logs process_time and `logger.warning(... heartbeat frame not received for more than {wait_time} seconds)` (lines 1170-1188)

claims.claim: monitor logs each frame's end-to-end traversal time
claims.found: line 1182-1183: process_time = (clock.get_time() - frame.timestamp)/1e9; logger.trace(... heartbeat frame processed in {process_time} seconds)

claims.claim: base_input.py:243 reads audio queue with asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=AUDIO_INPUT_TIMEOUT_SECS)
input watchdog 'once audio has started, warns on every 0.5 s gap with no frame (base_input.py:31,243,263)'
claims.found: line 263 is `except TimeoutError:` followed only by `if not audio_received: continue` (264-265); the file ENDS at line 266. There is NO warning emitted on an audio gap. grep for logger/warning in base_input.py returns only line 82 (debug 'Enabling audio on start'). The comment at line 246 says 'timeout should warn if there's no audio' but the warn is NOT implemented — the post-first-frame timeout path falls through silently to the loop top.
claims.correction: base_input.py emits no audio-gap warning. AUDIO_INPUT_TIMEOUT_SECS (0.5s) + wait_for exist, but the 'warns on every 0.5s gap' mechanism does not. Remove the 'warns' claim and the cite of :263 as a warning; the watchdog currently only sets a wait_for timeout with no alarm on gap.

claims.claim: Deepgram: 'Backend components should monitor audio flow and message cadence. If input or output stalls, the system should treat it as a failure and initiate recovery' (ebook 596-598)
claims.found: line 596: 'Backend components should monitor audio flow and message cadence.' line 597: 'If input or output stalls, the system should treat it as a failure and initiate' + 'recovery.' on next line. Exact match.

claims.claim: Vapi: 'sometimes a request to a provider just hangs — no error, no timeout, nothing' (~5% of turns, up to 5000 ms death spiral)
claims.found: line 32: 'Still ~5% of turns hung up to 5000ms ("death spiral"). Cause: sometimes a request to a provider just hangs — no error, no timeout, nothing.'

claims.claim: Vapi cure: hedged request on per-endpoint dynamic timeout (mean + k·σ), cancel-and-hedge to second-fastest
claims.found: line 34: 'cancel it and immediately fire a new request to the second-fastest ... Compute historical standard deviation per deployment, set a dynamic threshold'; line 43: 'Hedged requests with per-endpoint dynamic timeout (mean+k·σ)'.

claims.claim: LAT-06-hedged-requests neighbor referenced for the hedged-request cure
claims.found: Not opened; note only references it as supporting context, not as proof of a specific fact. Out of scope for refutation.

summary: 10 of 11 checkable assertions SUPPORTED. One WRONG: the note claims Pipecat's input-audio watchdog 'warns on every 0.5 s gap with no frame' citing base_input.py:263, but base_input.py implements no gap warning — line 263 is `except TimeoutError:` whose only body is `if not audio_received: continue`, the file ends at 266, and the sole logger call in the file is an unrelated debug at line 82. The 0.5s wait_for timeout (lines 31, 242-243) is real, but the alarm/warn on a stalled audio gap is absent (a comment at line 246 anticipates it but it is unimplemented). Deepgram quote, Vapi hang/5%/5000ms/mean+k·σ figures, and all heartbeat code_refs verified exact.
